children.heading: Historical park
children.label: 1 Historical park
children.text: The term “historical park” means the First State National Historical Park.

children.heading: Map
children.label: 2 Map
children.text: The term “map” means the map with pages numbered 1–6 entitled “First State National Historical Park, New Castle, Kent, Sussex Counties, DE and Delaware County, PA, Proposed Boundary”, numbered T19/80,000G, and dated October 2014.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Redesignation of First State National Monument
children.label: 1 Redesignation of First State National Monument

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The First State National Monument is redesignated as the First State National Historical Park, as generally depicted on the map.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: B Availability of funds
children.text: Any funds available for purposes of the First State National Monument shall be available for purposes of the historical park.

children.heading: References
children.label: C References
children.text: Any references in a law, regulation, document, record, map, or other paper of the United States to the First State National Monument shall be considered to be a reference to the historical park.

children.label: A
children.text: early Dutch, Swedish, and English settlement of the Colony of Delaware and portions of the Colony of Pennsylvania; and

children.label: i
children.text: in the birth of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: as the first State to ratify the Constitution.

children.label: A
children.text: Fort Christina National Historic Landmark in New Castle County, Delaware, as depicted on page 3 of 6 of the map.

children.label: B
children.text: Old Swedes Church National Historic Landmark in New Castle County, Delaware, as depicted on page 3 of 6 of the map.

children.label: C
children.text: John Dickinson Plantation National Historic Landmark in Kent County, Delaware, as depicted on page 5 of 6 of the map.

children.label: D
children.text: Ryves Holt House in Sussex County, Delaware, as depicted on page 6 of 6 of the map.

children.label: A
children.text: this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: the National Park System Organic Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 11See References in Text note below.1

children.label: ii
children.text: the Act of ( et seq.).August 21, 193516 U.S.C. 4611

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the Secretary may acquire all or a portion of any of the sites described in subsection (b)(3), including easements or other interests in land, by purchase from a willing seller, donation, or exchange.

children.heading: Donation only
children.label: ii Donation only
children.text: The Secretary may acquire only by donation all or a portion of the property identified as “Area for Potential Addition by Donation” on page 2 of 6 of the map.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: iii Limitation
children.text: No land or interest land may be acquired for inclusion in the historical park by condemnation.

children.heading: Boundary adjustment
children.label: B Boundary adjustment
children.text: On acquisition of land or an interest in land under subparagraph (A), the boundary of the historical park shall be adjusted to reflect the acquisition.

children.label: A
children.text: Fort Casimir;

children.label: B
children.text: DeVries Monument;

children.label: C
children.text: Amstel House;

children.label: D
children.text: Dutch House; and

children.label: E
children.text: Zwaanendael Museum.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into a cooperative agreement with the State of Delaware, political subdivisions of the State of Delaware, institutions of higher education, nonprofit organizations, and individuals to mark, interpret, and restore nationally significant historic or cultural resources within the boundaries of the historical park, if the cooperative agreement provides for reasonable public access to the resources.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: i Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the total cost of any activity carried out under a cooperative agreement entered into under subparagraph (A) shall be not more than 50 percent.

children.heading: Form of non-Federal share
children.label: ii Form of non-Federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share may be in the form of in-kind contributions or goods or services fairly valued.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 3 fiscal years after the date on which funds are made available to carry out this paragraph, the Secretary shall complete a management plan for the historical park.

children.heading: Applicable law
children.label: B Applicable law
children.text: The management plan shall be prepared in accordance with and other applicable laws.section 100502 of title 54

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the date on which funds are made available to carry out this section, the Secretary shall complete a study assessing the historical significance of additional properties in the State of Delaware that are associated with the purposes of historical park.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: 2 Requirements
children.text: The study prepared under paragraph (1) shall include an assessment of the potential for designating the additional properties as National Historic Landmarks.
